home.missionCard: Bridging medicine and technology — using research and software to understand disease more clearly and care for patients more effectively.

featuredProjects.title: Clinical Symptom Tracker
featuredProjects.description: A Python-based tool for logging and visualizing patient-reported symptoms over time, built to explore how software can support clinical decision-making.

featuredProjects.title: Alzheimer's Imaging Classifier
featuredProjects.description: An exploratory machine learning project applying image classification techniques to publicly available neuroimaging datasets.

featuredProjects.title: Volunteer Hours Dashboard
featuredProjects.description: A lightweight dashboard for tracking clinical volunteering and shadowing hours, built to practice full-stack development fundamentals.

news.title: Research Profile publication

news.description: Long-form writing on healthcare, medicine, and the research process begins soon.

news.description: Updates on current and upcoming science fair projects will be added as they develop.

about.bio: Akshath Karthik is a Healthcare Academy student, Class of 2029, pursuing a path toward medicine through a combination of coursework, clinical exposure, and independent research. Replace this paragraph with 2–3 sentences about your background, interests, and what draws you to healthcare and science.
